title: Isoptera lissophylla
intro: Isoptera lissophylla is a plant species commonly known as the "White Ant Tree" or "Barringtonia", which is a deciduous tree native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The leaves and bark of Isoptera lissophylla have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Isoptera lissophylla is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as a tincture or ointment to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and wounds.
